blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Neuroscience. That's specific.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's the point.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Aye — Maya Reeves. This is my boy Larry. He's good at math.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: He mentioned.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Oh you talked already?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: A little.
She caught up in the hallway. I wasn't sure if it was on purpose.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You just left.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I had another class.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: So did I.
She found me in the hallway anyway. I don't know how.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Good at math and neuroscience are not the same thing, by the way.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I didn't say they were.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: But you thought it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How do you know what I thought?

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Because everyone does. Math is numbers. Neuroscience is why numbers mean anything.
I didn't have anything to say to that. She said it like it was the most obvious thing in the world and I was the last one to arrive at it.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm just saying. You seem like someone who might actually get it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You've known me for one class period.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Yeah. But you listened when other people were talking, which is rarer than it sounds.

blocks.prompt: She said something real. Now it's your move.

options.label: "I just didn't want to talk over anyone."
"I don't know what my thing is yet."
